danger_signals.visual_form: Red symbiote coloration, demonic appearance contrasting Spider-Man's traditional red/blue
danger_signals.meaning: Primary existential threat; symbiote corruption of Cletus Kasady creates the game's antagonist force

danger_signals.visual_form: Underground environment, sewer level design, dark architectural markers
danger_signals.meaning: Carnage's power base and organized criminal infrastructure; danger intensifies in confined spaces

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual representation of Shriek (second-in-command), Demogoblin (corrupted Hobgoblin), Doppelganger (six-armed clone), Carrion (degraded clone)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat; multiple coordinated antagonists indicate world-state of organized supervillain coalition

danger_signals.visual_form: Mook enemies, generic criminal sprites responding to villain leadership
danger_signals.meaning: Carnage's influence spreads through the city; danger is both organized and distributed

safety_signals.visual_form: Playable character pairing; traditional Spider-Man (red/blue) alongside Venom (black/white symbiote)
safety_signals.meaning: Rivals overcome difference to form resistance; player agency through allied hero control

safety_signals.visual_form: Captain America, Black Cat, Iron Fist, Cloak and Dagger, Deathlok, Morbius, Firestar as collectible power-ups
safety_signals.meaning: Building a 'resistance' community; safety increases when allies are summoned to aid combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Semi-animated art adapted directly from comics; narrative framing device
safety_signals.meaning: Establishes heroic framework and legitimate authority against chaos; player is part of established heroic tradition

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban areas; symbiote's violent aesthetic mirrors Carnage threat but with redemptive arc

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red symbiote (distinct from Spider-Man red through demonic visual treatment)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Carnage symbiote form; corrupted/aggressive variant of symbiote aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Carnage as Cletus Kasady bonded with symbiote; represents failed redemption (Eddie Brock promised restraint, Carnage represents pure evil)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewers as faction base; criminal empire visual markers; demonic/chaotic environmental corruption

faction_indicators.color_identity: Mixed villain aesthetics; no unified color scheme
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual villain identities maintained: Shriek, Demogoblin, Doppelganger, Carrion
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demogoblin represents Hobgoblin's corruption into demonic form; visual tell of Carnage's corruptive power
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewer levels dominated by villain presence; criminal hideouts

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied; includes Captain America (blue/red/white), Black Cat (white/black), Iron Fist (green/yellow), Cloak and Dagger (dark/light contrast), etc.
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual hero identities; unified only by summoning mechanic and alliance purpose
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Summoned into combat zones; no permanent environmental claim except through temporary aid presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Separate hero identities in opening confrontation; traditional hero-vs-anti-hero color contrast
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spider-Man and Venom fight each other; both are dangerous threats in opening act

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red demonic symbiote form distinct from both Spider-Man and Venom; sewer environments become criminalcenters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced alliance between Spider-Man and Venom; enemy behavior shifts to organized villain coordination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple villain presence in sewers; escalating enemy density; villain team fully assembled (Shriek, Demogoblin, Doppelganger, Carrion visible)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Criminal mooks increase in number and coordination; villain lieutenants appear as mini-boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hero summon items scattered through levels; comic-styled cutscenes showing alliance formation; resistance headquarters visual implication
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players collect items to summon specific heroes; summoned ally behavior changes based on player character (Spider-Man vs Venom) summoning them

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Full hero roster appears alongside Spider-Man and Venom; Carnage visually dominant as final boss; sewer environment becomes all-or-nothing battle ground
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Carnage defeated but symbiote escapes; Spider-Man expresses hope for future Venom alliance; Venom retreats without commitment (open ending)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Carnage symbiote escape sequence; Venom departure; Spider-Man hopeful but alone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unresolved ally relationship; symbiote threat persists despite villain defeat; fragile stability

environmental_storytelling.example: Sewer levels (Carnage's criminal empire) use darker palette and constrained architecture vs. open city streets controlled by heroes
Doppelganger and other corrupted enemies show physical deformation contrasting with standard criminal mooks; symbiote influence visually evident on creatures
Semi-animated cutscenes using panels from actual Spider-Man comics establish narrative legitimacy and world continuity; player experiences adaptation of established canon
Specific items hidden in levels summon heroes; environmental placement implies hero intelligence networks and support systems; heroes appear in combat to validate player progress
City streets = hero domain (Spider-Man/Venom patrol); sewers = villain domain (Carnage empire); visual environment shift indicates narrative control shift
As player progresses, criminal mook encounters increase in number and coordination, visualizing Carnage's growing influence and criminal army expansion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fights Venom; encounters Carnage and nearly dies; demonstrates vulnerability and need for allies
Collaborates with Venom; collects summoning items; builds resistance network; more confident in group combat
Extends hope for future partnership with Venom; expresses gratitude; remains hopeful about redemption despite uncertain outcome
Claims promise to not use symbiote for evil; ambivalent about commitment to good; fights Spider-Man as rival
Desperate to stop Carnage (greater threat than Spider-Man rivalry); participates in organized resistance; shows competence in coordinated combat
Retreats before replying to Spider-Man's hope; ambiguous moral status maintained; symbiote escapes with Carnage symbiote; unresolved redemption arc
Escapes confrontation with Spider-Man and Venom; flees into city; demonstrates superior power and escape capability
Builds criminal empire in sewers; recruits villain lieutenants (Shriek as second-in-command, Demogoblin, Doppelganger, Carrion); establishes organized infrastructure
Inspires hundreds of criminals throughout city; multiplies threat through distributed mook army; launches organized attack on New York
Defeated by hero resistance despite superior organizational power; symbiote escapes even as Kasady is defeated (threat persistence)
Accepts Carnage's leadership; serves as lieutenant; visually distinct as sonic/psychic villain type

Attempts to join Carnage's team but is rejected; rejection triggers demonic transformation through unknown means; becomes Demogoblin; then hired by Carnage; visual tell of rejection leading to corruption
Restrained by Carnage; employed as mindless villain team member; represents symbiote's ability to control intelligent beings
In declining physical state; still hired by Carnage; represents symbiote's power to use even weakened beings
Hundreds swarm player; behavior becomes more organized as game progresses; visual tell of cascading criminal inspiration from villain leadership

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Summoned heroes (Captain America, Black Cat, Iron Fist, Cloak & Dagger, Deathlok, Morbius, Firestar)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Each hero responds to summoning in level-specific locations; effectiveness varies by which character (Spider-Man or Venom) summoned them; some heroes favor certain heroes

notes: Maximum Carnage is structured as a direct video game adaptation of a comic book story arc, making it one of the first games to adapt established narrative canon rather than original game plots. The visual communication of world-state relies heavily on: (1) faction color identity (Spider-Man red/blue vs. Venom black/white vs. Carnage demonic red), (2) environmental territory (city streets vs. sewers), (3) enemy composition escalation (rising mook density signals growing threat), (4) symbiote visual corruption as danger indicator, and (5) summoned ally presence as safety/hope signal. The game's red cartridge itself is a manufacturing-level visual signal of the game's violent content and thematic focus on Carnage. The narrative arc moves from rivalry (Spider-Man vs. Venom) → threat emergence (Carnage) → forced alliance → organized resistance → ambiguous victory (symbiote escapes, Venom's commitment unresolved). Political situation is communicated through faction organization: Carnage builds a hierarchical criminal empire (Shriek second-in-command, hired lieutenants), while heroes operate through decentralized resistance network. Demogoblin's transformation from rejected applicant to Carnage's employ represents the political danger of exclusion and corruption. The game's teen rating (Genesis MA-13) indicates visual violence is a core communication method of danger.
